title_en: Introduction to Nayl al-Awtar by ash-Shawkani
content_en:
Nayl al-Awtar min Ahadith Sayyid al-Akhyar Sharh Muntaqa al-Akhbar ('Attaining the Goals from the Hadiths of the Best of Men: Commentary on Muntaqa al-Akhbar') is the most celebrated work of hadith-based jurisprudence in the post-classical Islamic tradition, authored by the Yemeni scholar Muhammad ibn 'Ali ibn Muhammad ash-Shawkani (1173–1250 AH / 1759–1834 CE).

Ash-Shawkani was born and spent his life in Yemen, rising to become the chief judge (qadi al-qudat) of Sana'a. He began his scholarly career within the Zaydi school of fiqh dominant in Yemen, but through his study of hadith, legal theory, and the works of scholars like Ibn Hazm and Ibn Taymiyya, he came to reject strict madhab-following (taqlid) in favor of independent legal reasoning (ijtihad) directly from the Quran and Sunnah. His scholarly position — sometimes called 'neo-ijtihad' — influenced later reform movements in the Islamic world.

Muntaqa al-Akhbar by Ibn Taymiyya's grandfather Majd ad-Din Ibn Taymiyya (d. 652 AH) is a collection of hadiths relevant to legal rulings, organized by legal topic. It differs from the hadith collections (like the Six Books) in its explicit legal orientation: each chapter presents the hadiths relevant to a legal question, allowing the jurist to assess the full hadith evidence for that question. Ash-Shawkani's Nayl al-Awtar is a running commentary on Muntaqa al-Akhbar, for each hadith explaining its chain of transmission, assessing its authenticity, extracting the legal rulings contained in it, and comparing the positions of the four Sunni schools.

The methodology of Nayl al-Awtar is explicitly comparative and hadith-centered. For each legal question, ash-Shawkani presents the positions of all four Sunni schools, evaluates the hadith evidence for each, and identifies what he considers the most evidentially grounded position — which may or may not coincide with any particular school's position. This approach gives Nayl al-Awtar the character of a hadith-based comparative fiqh encyclopedia.

Ash-Shawkani's other major works include Irshad al-Fuhul (on usul al-fiqh) and al-Fath ar-Rabbani (a collection of his fatwas). Together with Nayl al-Awtar, these works established ash-Shawkani as one of the most influential scholars of the thirteenth-century AH reform movement, alongside scholars like Ibn 'Abd al-Wahhab in Arabia and Shah Wali Allah in India.

For the contemporary student of Islamic law, Nayl al-Awtar is invaluable as a one-stop reference for the hadith evidence behind legal rulings and the scholarly debates surrounding them. Its comprehensive comparative approach and ash-Shawkani's clear writing style make it accessible to advanced students across all schools.
content_id:
Nayl al-Awtar min Ahadith Sayyid al-Akhyar Sharh Muntaqa al-Akhbar ('Meraih Tujuan dari Hadis-Hadis Pemimpin Para Orang Terbaik: Komentar atas Muntaqa al-Akhbar') adalah karya fikih berbasis hadis yang paling terkenal dalam tradisi Islam pasca-klasik, ditulis oleh ulama Yaman Muhammad ibn 'Ali ibn Muhammad asy-Syawkani (1173–1250 H / 1759–1834 M).

Asy-Syawkani lahir dan menghabiskan hidupnya di Yaman, kemudian naik menjadi hakim agung (qadi al-qudat) Sana'a. Beliau memulai karier keilmuannya dalam mazhab Zaydi yang dominan di Yaman, namun melalui kajiannya tentang hadis, teori hukum, dan karya-karya ulama seperti Ibn Hazm dan Ibn Taimiyah, beliau sampai pada penolakan terhadap taklid ketat kepada mazhab demi ijtihad independen langsung dari Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah. Posisi keilmuannya — yang kadang disebut 'neo-ijtihad' — memengaruhi gerakan-gerakan pembaruan di dunia Islam di kemudian hari.

Muntaqa al-Akhbar karya kakek Ibn Taimiyah, Majd ad-Din Ibn Taimiyah (w. 652 H), adalah kumpulan hadis yang relevan dengan hukum-hukum fiqih, diatur berdasarkan topik hukum. Berbeda dari kitab-kitab hadis (seperti Kutub as-Sittah) dalam orientasi hukumnya yang eksplisit: setiap bab menyajikan hadis-hadis yang relevan untuk suatu permasalahan hukum, memungkinkan ahli hukum menilai seluruh bukti hadis untuk pertanyaan tersebut. Nayl al-Awtar karya asy-Syawkani adalah komentar yang mengalir atas Muntaqa al-Akhbar, menjelaskan sanad setiap hadis, menilai keshahihannya, mengekstrak hukum-hukum yang terkandung di dalamnya, dan membandingkan posisi keempat mazhab Sunni.

Metodologi Nayl al-Awtar secara eksplisit bersifat komparatif dan berpusat pada hadis. Untuk setiap permasalahan hukum, asy-Syawkani menyajikan posisi semua mazhab empat, mengevaluasi bukti hadis untuk masing-masing, dan mengidentifikasi posisi yang menurut beliau paling kuat secara evidensial — yang mungkin sejalan atau tidak sejalan dengan posisi mazhab mana pun. Pendekatan ini memberi Nayl al-Awtar karakter sebuah kajian hukum yang komprehensif, jauh melampaui sekedar penjelasan satu mazhab.
content_ar:
«نيل الأوطار من أحاديث سيد الأخيار شرح منتقى الأخبار» من أبرز كتب شرح الحديث الفقهي وأكثرها أثرًا في تيارات الإصلاح الإسلامي الحديثة. وهو شرح على «منتقى الأخبار» لابن تيمية الجد، الذي جمع فيه الأحاديث التي يحتج بها الفقهاء في المسائل الخلافية.

والشوكاني (1173-1250هـ) عالم يمني متعدد المواهب جمع بين الإمامة في الحديث والفقه والأصول مع نزوع واضح نحو الاجتهاد ورفض التقليد الأعمى. وقد أتاح له هذا المزج ذبَّ الأسانيد ونقدَها ثم استخلاص الأحكام الفقهية منها في كتاب جمع بين التخريج والفقه المقارن في آنٍ واحد.

والكتاب في حجمه ومادته من أضخم كتب شروح الحديث الفقهي؛ ويُعدّ مرجعًا لا يُستغنى عنه في الفقه المقارن والبحث الحديثي لكل من يريد الوقوف على الدليل الحديثي الفقهي من مختلف المذاهب في مكان واحد.
